items.title: Migrations apply and roll back cleanly (scout_cache + scout_jobs)
items.precondition: Docker Postgres up on :8765; prior migrations applied.
steps: 1. npm run migrate

2. Inspect \d scout_cache — columns id, session_id (FK→interview_sessions ON DELETE CASCADE), provider, repo_ref, findings jsonb, created_at timestamptz; index on session_id; CHECK provider IN (github,azure).

3. Inspect \d scout_jobs — columns id, session_id (FK→interview_sessions ON DELETE CASCADE), provider, repo_ref, status, attempts, last_error, created_at/updated_at timestamptz; indexes on (status) and (session_id); CHECK status IN (pending,running,done,failed) and provider IN (github,azure).
4. npm run migrate:rollback — scout_jobs + its constraints dropped (Batch 5).
5. npm run migrate — scout_jobs restored.
items.expected: Both tables exist with their FKs (ON DELETE CASCADE), filter indexes (§10.4), and CHECK constraints; the scout_jobs migration rolls back and re-applies cleanly.
items.evidence: Batch 5 run: 1 migration (scout_jobs). \d scout_jobs showed all 9 columns + scout_jobs_status_index + scout_jobs_session_id_index + scout_jobs_status_check (pending/running/done/failed) + scout_jobs_provider_check (github/azure) + FK ON DELETE CASCADE. migrate:rollback: Batch 5 rolled back; to_regclass('public.scout_jobs') => null. migrate: Batch 5 run again; scout_jobs restored, 0 rows. scout_cache unchanged from spec 5.
items.notes: Postgres-only by design; no mssql.sql (mirrors foundation). New migration: 20260624000005_scout_jobs_schema.ts.

items.title: ScoutJobModel: enqueue, race-safe claim, mark done, bounded-retry → dead-letter (§21)
items.precondition: Unit suite against the real test DB (vitest globalSetup runs migrations).
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/models/ScoutJobModel.test.ts
2. Assert enqueue creates a pending row with attempts 0; findLatestBySession returns the newest job.
3. Assert claimNextPending marks the job running and burns one attempt at claim time.
4. Assert two CONCURRENT claims of one pending job hand it to exactly one (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED) — the other gets null; the job advances exactly once.
5. Assert recordFailure re-queues below SCOUT_JOB.MAX_ATTEMPTS, then dead-letters (status failed) at the cap.
items.expected: The queue is durable and race-safe; a job is never double-claimed; bounded retries end in a dead-letter terminal state, never a silent drop (§21.1, §21.2).
items.evidence: 7 tests passed in ScoutJobModel.test.ts, including the concurrent-claim test (exactly one claim of the job, one null; attempts==1 after) and the re-queue-then-dead-letter test (pending below cap, failed at cap).
items.notes: Race-safety uses SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED inside a transaction (BaseModel.runTransaction).

items.title: ScoutJobProcessor: claim → run via CodeScoutService → cache + mark done; idempotent; failure path (§21.3/§21.1/§21.2)
items.precondition: Unit suite; CodeScoutAgent.run mocked (no live provider/model); real DB/models/cache.
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/services/scoutJobs/ScoutJobProcessor.test.ts
2. Assert processNext claims a pending job, runs the scan through CodeScoutService.runScout, writes scout_cache, and marks the job done.
3. Assert idempotency: a job whose session is ALREADY cached marks done WITHOUT re-running the agent (read-through short-circuit, §21.1).
4. Assert an empty queue is a clean no-op; drain processes every pending job.

5. Assert the failure path increments attempts and re-queues, then dead-letters at SCOUT_JOB.MAX_ATTEMPTS; processNext never throws to the caller (a bad job doesn't crash the loop); last_error stores the typed code, not a stack (§3.4).
items.expected: The processor orchestrates and reuses the service (§21.3 — no scan logic of its own); a repeat run is safe (§21.1); failures are bounded and dead-lettered with context (§21.2, §21.4).
items.evidence: 6 tests passed in ScoutJobProcessor.test.ts: claim→cache→done; idempotent no-rescan when cache present; empty-queue no-op; failure re-queue→dead-letter across MAX_ATTEMPTS with attempts==3 and status failed; processNext resolves (does not reject) on failure; drain clears 2 jobs.
items.notes: The processor reconstructs the session owner via InterviewSessionModel.findByIdSystem (trusted-processor path) so runScout still owner-verifies (§11.7).

items.title: Scan endpoint: POST ENQUEUES (202, no inline scan) + GET status returns findings once done; idempotent re-POST; error/owner paths
items.precondition: App over supertest; CodeScoutAgent.run mocked; owner via x-dev-user-id header.
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/controllers/codeScout/CodeScoutController.test.ts
2. POST /sessions/:id/scout {provider,repoRef} → 202 envelope with jobId, status pending, alreadyComplete false, and the AGENT NOT CALLED (no inline scan).

3. GET /sessions/:id/scout → status pending + no findings before processing; run ScoutJobProcessor.drain; GET again → status done + findings (verifyWithEngineering true); agent called exactly once (by the worker).
4. Re-POST after completion → 202, alreadyComplete true, jobId null, agent STILL called once (idempotent short-circuit, §21.1).
5. Missing/unknown provider/repoRef → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (no job enqueued); missing/other-owner session on POST and GET → 404 SESSION_NOT_FOUND.
items.expected: {success,data,error} envelope on every path (§8.1); the request enqueues and returns 202 WITHOUT scanning (the scan runs only in the processor); validation at the boundary; owner isolation on both enqueue and status (§11.7).
items.evidence: 9 tests passed in CodeScoutController.test.ts, including the 202-no-inline-scan assertion (mockRun not called on POST), the pending→done status transition after drain, the idempotent re-POST (mockRun still once), and the 400/404 boundary + owner paths on both endpoints.
items.notes: Replaces the spec-5 inline-scan controller tests; the read-through guarantee is now proven across enqueue → process → status.

items.title: Guarded internal processor trigger for Vercel Cron (§5.4, §21)
items.precondition: App over supertest; CodeScoutAgent.run mocked; SCOUT_WORKER_SECRET set by vitest config.
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/controllers/codeScout/InternalScoutController.test.ts
2. POST /internal/scout/process with NO secret header → 401 SCOUT_TRIGGER_AUTH_FAILED (fail-closed).
3. POST with the WRONG secret → 401.
4. POST with the CORRECT secret and a pending job → 200, data.processed==1, job marked done.
5. POST with the correct secret and an empty queue → 200, data.processed==0.
items.expected: The trigger is machine-to-machine and guarded by a shared secret (§5.4); it refuses unauthenticated callers fail-closed; with the secret it drains the queue and reports the count, in the {success,data,error} envelope.
items.evidence: 4 tests passed in InternalScoutController.test.ts: no-secret 401, wrong-secret 401, correct-secret drains 1 job (done), correct-secret empty queue processed 0.
items.notes: Endpoint POST /internal/scout/process; header x-scout-worker-secret. Local driver `npm run scout:work` exercises the same processor in-process without the HTTP path; verified it boots and polls.

items.title: Spec 6 (grounded options): grounded path AND no-findings fallback both still work with backgrounding
items.precondition: Unit suite; interview agent mocked; scout_cache seeded directly for the grounded case.
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/controllers/interview/feature-services/InterviewEngineService.test.ts
2. Assert the no-findings fallback: with no scout_cache row, generation forwards findings=undefined and persists ungrounded options (the path taken while a background scan is still pending).
3. Assert the grounded path: with a scout_cache row present, generation forwards the cached findings and persists grounded options + skips.
4. Confirm the engine reads scout_cache via the model (unchanged by backgrounding) — the job populates the cache the engine already reads.
items.expected: Generation is grounded when findings are cached and falls back to ungrounded when they are absent; backgrounding only changes WHEN the cache is populated (out of band), not how spec 6 reads it.
items.evidence: 16 tests passed in InterviewEngineService.test.ts, including the no-findings fallback (params.findings undefined → ungrounded options) and the grounded path (params.findings defined → grounded options + skips). The live run confirmed status is `pending` with no findings before processing (fallback window) and `done` with findings after (grounded thereafter).
items.notes: No change to InterviewEngineService.findCachedFindings; it reads ScoutCacheModel directly, which the processor writes.

items.title: LIVE background run against a small public repo: enqueue fast → process out of band → cached
items.precondition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set server-side; network access; no GITHUB_TOKEN (public repo).
steps: 1. Seed a session with the request 'Add support for configuring allowed CORS origins from a database at runtime.'
2. CodeScoutService.enqueueScan against expressjs/cors — confirm it returns FAST with a job id (no inline scan).
3. Confirm status is `pending` with no findings before the processor runs.
4. Run ScoutJobProcessor.drain once — the REAL Git Trees/Contents API + real claude-opus-4-8 scan happens here, within the caps.
5. Confirm scout_cache written, job status=done (attempts 1, no error), and the status endpoint returns done + findings tagged verify.
items.expected: Enqueue returns in milliseconds (the request no longer carries the scan); the bounded scan runs out of band in the processor; one scout_cache row persists; the job completes; the status endpoint reports done with coarse, verify-tagged findings.
items.evidence: Repo expressjs/cors (public). enqueue returned in 5ms, jobId 37, status pending, alreadyComplete false. Status before processing: pending, findings absent. drain processed 1 job in 15166ms; logs showed searches:6 (=cap), filesRead:8 (=cap, from 11 paths). Job 37: status done, attempts 1, last_error null. Status endpoint: done, findings present, verifyWithEngineering true, 3 coarse areas (Origin resolution/middleware engine S/clear, Documentation XS/clear, Database integration M/uncertain); the model noted the dynamic-origin-function already exists and the README documents the pattern. enqueue 5ms << scan 15166ms confirms the scan is no longer on the request path. Synthetic rows cleaned via ON DELETE CASCADE; harness scripts/live-scout-job.ts removed after the run.
items.notes: This is the backgrounding proof: the ~15s scan that previously risked a Vercel function timeout now runs in the Cron-processed worker, not the request.

items.title: Toolchain gates: tsc (BE+FE), lint (BE+FE), check:conventions --strict, full test suite
steps: 1. npx tsc --noEmit (backend) and (cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit)
2. npm run lint (backend) and (cd frontend && npm run lint)
3. bash scripts/check-conventions.sh --strict
4. npm run test (backend) and (cd frontend && npm run test)
items.expected: tsc zero errors both trees; lint clean both trees; 0 backend hard violations; all suites green (no regressions) + the new job/processor/trigger tests.
items.evidence: Backend tsc: 0. Frontend tsc: 0. Backend lint: clean. Frontend lint: clean. check:strict: 0 backend hard violations (13 advisory: pre-existing FE/TicketComment + the standard migration .raw §10.2 advisory, now incl. scout_jobs' CHECK DDL — same allowed pattern as scout_cache). Backend test: 22 files, 155 tests passed (138 baseline + 17 new: 7 ScoutJobModel + 6 ScoutJobProcessor + 4 InternalScoutController; CodeScoutController rewritten to the background flow). Frontend test: 2 passed. No regressions; spec 6's 16 engine tests green.
